description:
You are a web developer who just cloned a new project into /home/user/webapp.  
Before you can start coding, you need a clean Python virtual environment created with the
standard library module “venv”, and you must document exactly what you did.

Your job
1. Inside /home/user/webapp create a virtual-environment directory named “venv”.
2. Activate that environment and install the precise package version “Flask==2.2.5”.
3. Generate a plain-text log file called /home/user/webapp/setup.log that lists
   every terminal command you executed to achieve steps 1 and 2, **one command per line,
   in the exact order you ran them**.  
   • The log must end with a single trailing newline.  
   • No timestamps, prompts, comments, extra blank lines or wrapping—just the raw
     commands you typed.

Required output files & structure
/home/user/webapp/venv/         (directory created by python -m venv)
/home/user/webapp/venv/pyvenv.cfg   (file generated by venv)
/home/user/webapp/requirements.txt  (generated by “pip freeze”, must list Flask only)
/home/user/webapp/setup.log         (your command log)

Automated tests will verify:
• “venv” exists and is a valid virtual environment (pyvenv.cfg must contain the line  
  “include-system-site-packages = false”).
• /home/user/webapp/requirements.txt exists and its **entire contents** are exactly:  
  Flask==2.2.5⏎
• /home/user/webapp/setup.log exists and contains exactly four lines, each terminated by
  a single newline, in this order:

  python3 -m venv venv  
  source venv/bin/activate  
  pip install Flask==2.2.5  
  pip freeze > requirements.txt⏎

(No spaces before/after the commands, no prompt symbols.)

Important: you do not need root privileges and should work entirely under /home/user.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. Directory: /home/user/webapp/venv/
   – Created by “python3 -m venv venv”.
   – Must contain the standard sub-directories bin/, lib/, include/, and file pyvenv.cfg.
   – /home/user/webapp/venv/pyvenv.cfg must have the entry:
     include-system-site-packages = false

2. File: /home/user/webapp/requirements.txt  
   Exact contents (one line, newline at EOL):
   Flask==2.2.5\n

3. File: /home/user/webapp/setup.log  
   Exact contents (four lines, newline after each line):
   python3 -m venv venv\n
   source venv/bin/activate\n
   pip install Flask==2.2.5\n
   pip freeze > requirements.txt\n
